content: We can frame the performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and heuristic curious agents as an active learning problem for the agent’s own episodic memory: curiosity-driven exploration is only useful when the agent actively selects which unexpected surprise memories to retain and rehearse, rather than storing all encountered novelty. State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active memory architectures can be adapted to prune low-information curiosity bonuses to resolve longstanding issues of RND getting stuck in noisy, high-novelty trivial environments.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
